# Research — 2026-05-29

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills)

## Executive Summary

- **4 of 5 items from 2026-05-19 are now resolved** in our codebase: MCP paginated `tools/list` (`src/mcp/client.ts:64-76` loops on `nextCursor` with a `MAX_PAGES=100` cap), startup API connectivity timeout (`src/providers/connectivity.ts` + `StartupTimeoutError` in `src/providers/auth.ts`), rewind / "Summarize up to here" (`src/command/rewind.ts`, `src/cli/tui/hooks/useRewind.ts`), and prompt-history consecutive-duplicate suppression (`src/cli/tui/components/InputBox.tsx:222`).
- **2 items still open from prior research**: goal-driven autonomous loop (`/goal`) and per-`execute()` agent usage reset.
- **anthropics/claude-code v2.1.156** (May 28): Opus 4.8 thinking-block fix; **v2.1.154** (May 28) shipped Opus 4.8 (default to high effort), **dynamic workflows** (orchestrate tens-to-hundreds of background agents via `/workflows`), context-aware question reservation ("only ask when Claude genuinely cannot decide"), and renamed `/effort` slider labels Faster/Smarter.
- **claude-code v2.1.152**: `disallowed-tools` frontmatter for skills/commands, `/reload-skills` command, new `MessageDisplay` hook event that lets hooks transform or hide assistant message text as it is displayed, `SessionStart` hook can now set `sessionTitle` and request `reloadSkills`, `--fallback-model` automatic switch when primary model is not found.
- **claude-code v2.1.149**: `/usage` per-category breakdown (skills, subagents, plugins, MCP server cost). v2.1.147: `/code-review` was renamed and simplified to a correctness-bug review at a chosen effort level (`/code-review high`), with `--comment` posting findings to GitHub PRs.
- **claude-code v2.1.145**: `claude agents --json` for scripting (status bars, tmux-resurrect), Read tool now returns a "PARTIAL view" page instead of a hard error when a whole-file read exceeds the token limit, GFM task-list checkboxes rendered in markdown.
- **cline/cline** (May 22-29): SAP AI Core moved to AI SDK community provider (`fix: SAP AI Core uses AI SDK community provider (CLINE-2307)`), Discord connector (sessions bound to individual message authors), Claude Opus 4.8 provider support, Moonshot Kimi K2.6 + DeepSeek V4 + GPT-5.5 model entries, VCR provider smoke tests to prevent ChatGPT regressions, **global AGENTS rules support in the SDK** (`feat(sdk): support global AGENTS rules`).
- **Kilo-Org/kilocode** (May 28): Italian language support, DeepSeek surfaced in popular providers, JetBrains polish; otherwise an Effect-facade migration for SessionSummary/Project/ToolRegistry — internal refactor, no user-facing impact.
- **aider-ai/aider** & **continuedev/continue**: dormant — only model-list expansions on aider, no continue commits since April.
- **Key new gap**: `/code-review` command — no equivalent in our CLI; `src/skill/skills/index.ts:11` defines a `code-review` *skill* but not a first-class command that runs at a chosen effort and can post inline GitHub PR comments.
- **Key new gap**: `--fallback-model` automatic switch. We have `fallbackModel: 'gpt-4o'` in `src/config/routingConfig.ts:97`, but it is only consulted by the score-based router (`src/core/router.ts:207`), never by the provider layer when the configured primary model is missing/unavailable; an unknown model still results in a hard error rather than a graceful fallback for the rest of the session.

## Detailed Findings

### What was built since 2026-05-19 (closed gaps)
- MCP `nextCursor` loop in `src/mcp/client.ts:64-76` with `MAX_PAGES=100` safety cap — closes the pagination gap from v2.1.144.
- `src/providers/connectivity.ts` + `StartupTimeoutError` exported from `src/providers/index.ts:15` — closes the 75s startup hang from v2.1.144.
- `src/command/rewind.ts` + `useRewind.ts` hook — closes "Summarize up to here" (v2.1.141).
- `InputBox.tsx:222` "skip consecutive identical entries" — closes prompt-history dedup (v2.1.147).

### Still open from 2026-05-19
- `/goal` autonomous loop — no command, no evaluator. Orchestrator (`src/core/orchestrator.ts`) is single-turn.
- Agent usage reset — `src/agent/index.ts` accumulates usage across `execute()` calls instead of resetting per dispatch (cline v3.0.7 fix).

### New gaps (sorted by leverage)

#### 1. `/code-review` command (Category: command — medium, 1-2 days)
- **What**: A `/code-review [effort]` command that runs a structured correctness-bug review over uncommitted changes (or `git diff base...HEAD`) at a chosen effort level. Optional `--fix` applies safe simplification/efficiency suggestions; optional `--comment` posts findings as inline GitHub PR comments via `gh api`.
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.147 renamed `/simplify` → `/code-review` and v2.1.152 added `--fix` apply-mode; v2.1.154 then split it back so `/simplify` does cleanup-only and `/code-review --fix` does the bug hunt — this is now their flagship review flow. Our `src/skill/skills/index.ts:11` has a `code-review` skill but it is a passive prompt template — there is no command that bundles diff selection, effort tuning, fix application, or PR-comment posting. Users currently re-prompt manually after every commit.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.147–v2.1.154 `/code-review`, `/code-review --fix`, `/code-review --comment`.
- **Size**: medium (1-2 days)

#### 2. `--fallback-model` automatic provider switch (Category: provider — small, < 4h)
- **What**: When `getProviderForModel(modelId)` rejects an unknown model id (or the API returns a 4xx model-not-found), automatically switch to `routingConfig.preferences.fallbackModel` for the rest of the session and surface a one-line warning in the TUI. Today the call throws and the user has to restart.
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.152: "Claude Code now switches to your configured `--fallback-model` for the rest of the session when the primary model is not found, instead of failing every request." We already have `fallbackModel: 'gpt-4o'` in `src/config/routingConfig.ts:97` but `src/providers/index.ts` does not consult it; an env-var typo or a renamed deployment id breaks every turn until the user fixes config.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.152.
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

#### 3. Read tool PARTIAL-view fallback (Category: tool — small, < 4h)
- **What**: When `read.ts` is called without `offset`/`limit` on a file whose serialized size exceeds the token budget, return the first page with `truncated: true` and a "PARTIAL view — call read again with offset=N to continue" hint, instead of relying on the caller to know the limit. The streaming reader at `src/tool/tools/read.ts:54-83` already supports byte caps; the wrapper at line 154 just needs a budget check before returning.
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.145: "Improved the Read tool to return a truncated first page with a 'PARTIAL view' notice instead of a hard error when a whole-file read exceeds the token limit." Today our `read` tool returns the whole file then truncates the rendered output via `truncateOutput`, which loses line-number context for big files. A page-based fallback is friendlier for the model and lets it resume cleanly.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.145.
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

#### 4. `disallowed-tools` frontmatter for skills and slash commands (Category: tool — small, < 4h)
- **What**: Honor a `disallowed-tools: [bash, write]` (or YAML list) frontmatter key in skill markdown (`SkillSchema` in `src/skill/index.ts:14`) and command markdown. When the skill is active, those tools are removed from the model's tool list for the duration of the active turn. The skill loader already parses frontmatter; we'd add the field to the Zod schema and have `streamingOrchestrator.ts` filter `tools` accordingly.
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.152: "Skills and slash commands can now set `disallowed-tools` in frontmatter to remove tools from the model while the skill is active." This is the natural counterpart to allowlisting and prevents skills from doing things they shouldn't (e.g., a `code-review` skill should not call `write` or `bash`). It is also a permission surface that skill authors can self-govern with, reducing prompt-injection blast radius.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.152.
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

#### 5. `agents --json` for scripting (Category: command — small, < 4h)
- **What**: Add `--json` to `alexi sessions list` (or `alexi agent list`) so the output is a stable JSON array of `{id, title, model, cwd, lastActiveAt, status}` per session, suitable for tmux status bars, custom dashboards, and shell pipelines.
- **Why**: Claude Code v2.1.145: "Added `claude agents --json` to list live Claude sessions as JSON for scripting (tmux-resurrect, status bars, session pickers)." Our `src/cli/commands/sessions.ts` only renders a human table. Power users running multiple parallel sessions (e.g. via the agent-manager workflow described in AGENTS.md) need machine-readable session metadata to build their own picker UIs without scraping.
- **Source**: anthropics/claude-code v2.1.145.
- **Size**: small (< 4h)

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Pick up the two carry-overs first** (`/goal` and per-execute usage reset). They were already specified, scoped, and source-cited in `2026-05-19-research.md`. Closing them keeps research-to-implementation latency honest.
2. **Ship `--fallback-model` next** (#2 above): smallest effort, biggest reliability win. Wire `getProviderForModel()` to consult `routingConfig.preferences.fallbackModel` on `Unknown model` and emit a single warning. Add a regression test in `src/providers/index.test.ts` (mock `getProviderForModel` with a typo'd id).
3. **Then `disallowed-tools` and `--json`** in parallel — both are < 4h and unblock skill authors / external tooling respectively.
4. **`/code-review` is the medium-effort feature for this cycle** (1-2 days). Split into two PRs: (a) the command + diff selection + effort plumbing reusing the existing `code-review` skill prompt; (b) the `--comment` GitHub integration via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{N}/comments`.
5. **Defer Opus 4.8 / dynamic workflows / `/workflows`**: model rollout is upstream-driven and dynamic workflows are an Anthropic-managed orchestration product; their scope (hundreds of background agents, fast-mode pricing) is outside our SAP-AI-Core, single-binary CLI envelope. Track via routing config (`src/config/routingConfig.ts:42`) when SAP exposes the deployment.
6. **Watch for `MessageDisplay` hook**: not yet justified for our codebase (we don't have message-mutation use cases) but it's the kind of feature that lands in the next layer of skills authors needing privacy/redaction control. Re-evaluate next cycle if hook usage grows.
